label: Simple skin avulsion, skin tear, or abrasion without repair need
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple skin abrasion, skin tear, or skin avulsion.
Clinician documents no repair need and no retained foreign body, deep structure injury, infection concern, or high-risk wound pathway.
Wound was cleaned and dressed with clinician-entered wound care instructions.
exclusion_criteria: Wound needing sutures, staples, glue, operative repair, or laceration pathway.

Burn, chemical exposure, high-pressure injection injury, crush injury, open fracture, joint violation, tendon/nerve/vascular concern, retained foreign body, bite wound, dirty wound, or infected wound.
Diabetic foot wound, immunocompromised host, pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed wound plan.
must_not_miss: Tendon, nerve, vascular, or joint injury.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, wound clinic, or the ED in 2 to 3 days if the wound is not improving, or sooner if your clinician gave a specific schedule.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports cuts, punctures, skin breaks, wound healing, infection warning signs, and escalation for deep, dirty, bite, puncture, foot, or high-risk wounds. ED-specific decisions about repair need, tetanus, topical agents, and wound severity require clinician-entered context.

terms: cleaned and dressed with wound care instructions
no foreign body deep structure infection concern
no retained foreign body deep structure injury infection concern

review.notes: Phase 204 clinician review applied from multi-phenotype packet. Draft-only until final approval after runtime stress. No static tetanus or topical antibiotic language.
